steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank fills the subject of “found” and consists of a title (US biologist) directly followed by a proper name (Brad Gemmell), then “and colleagues.” The name identifies which biologist is being referenced.

steps.title: Apply appositive punctuation rules
steps.content:
A restrictive appositive—one that provides essential information to identify a noun—should not be set off with commas.

Here, Brad Gemmell is restrictive; it specifies which US biologist, so no comma separates the title from the name.

Those choices incorrectly treat the name as a nonrestrictive appositive requiring commas on both sides.

Choice D inconsistently places a comma only before “and colleagues” without matching punctuation around the name.

The correct answer correctly uses no punctuation between the title and name.

That confirms B is correct.
